out of list, main landmark, heading, level 1, W 3C Web Accessibility Initiative Home
Making the Web Accessible, region, heading, level 2, Making the Web Accessible
Strategies, standards, and supporting resources to help you make the Web more accessible to people with disabilities.
heading, level 3, W 3C
The World Wide Web Consortium (W 3C)
develops international standards for the
Web: HTML, CSS, and many more.
heading, level 3, WAI
The W 3C Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI)
develops standards and support materials
to help you understand and implement
accessibility.
heading, level 3, You
You can use W 3C WAI resources to make
your websites, applications, and other
digital creations more accessible and
usable to everyone.

heading, level 3, For Review: WCAG 3 Working Draft – March 2026
link, W 3C Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 3.0 Working Draft, is updated. The starting place
for background, timeline, and up to date information is the, link, WCAG 3 Introduction, dot For a
list of changes in the March 2026 draft, see, link, Status: In progress drafts, dot
heading, level 3, For Review: W 3C Accessibility Guidelines Evaluation Methodology (WCAG
heading, level 3, EM) 2.0 First Draft Note
link, W 3C Accessibility Guidelines Evaluation Methodology (WCAG EM) 2.0, is available as a
first Draft Note. WCAG EM describes a methodology with a step by step process to
evaluate how well digital products conform to Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
(WCAG) 2. WCAG EM 1 is specifically for testing websites and web pages. WCAG EM 2
also applies to apps and other digital products. For an introduction to WCAG EM and
more information about the WCAG EM 2 draft, see, link, WCAG EM Overview, dot
heading, level 3, For Review: Cognitive Accessibility Research Modules First Draft Notes
link, Cognitive Accessibility Research Modules, are available as first Draft Notes. The
documents cover research on accessibility for people with cognitive and learning
disabilities for, link, Voice Systems and Conversational Interfaces, ,, link, Technology Assisted
link, Indoor Navigation slash Wayfinding, ,, link, Online Safety and Wellbeing (Algorithms and Data), ,
and, link, Supported Decision Making Online, dot They address user needs, accessibility issues,
directions for solutions, and areas for further research. We particularly seek input on
what might be appropriate to add to these documents, including other relevant
research, additional user needs, and the connection between research and priorities
for new work in W 3C groups. Information on related work is in, link, Cognitive Accessibility at
link, W 3C, dot
heading, level 3, ACT Rules Format 1.1 is a Web Standard (W 3C Recommendation)
link, Accessibility Conformance Testing (ACT) Rules Format 1.1, is now a web standard (W 3C
Recommendation). ACT Rules Format defines a format for writing accessibility test
rules. The test rules can be used for developing automated testing tools and manual
testing methodologies. For an introduction to ACT Rules, see, link, Accessibility Conformance
link, Testing (ACT) Overview, dot
heading, level 3, WCAG 2.2 Approved as an ISO Standard
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (, link, WCAG, ) 2.2 is now an approved International
Organization for Standardization (ISO) standard: ISO slash IEC 40500:2025. It is available free
from, link, ISO slash IEC 40500:2025 Information technology W 3C Web Content Accessibility
link, Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2, dot This standard enables more countries to formally adopt WCAG
2.2. Additional information is in the, link, W 3C press release on WCAG 2.2 approved as
link, ISO slash IEC international standard, and, link, ISO slash IEC 40500, EAA, EN 301 549’ section of WCAG 2
link, Overview, dot
heading, level 3, For Review: WCAG 3 Working Draft September 2025
link, W 3C Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 3.0 Working Draft, is updated. For review questions
and how to comment, please see the, link, WCAG 3 section About this draft’, dot The starting
place for background, timeline, and up to date information is the, link, WCAG 3 Introduction, dot
heading, level 3, For Wide Review: Accessibility Maturity Model Updated Draft Note
link, Accessibility Maturity Model Updated Draft Note, is ready for review. The Accessibility
Maturity Model provides a framework to assess an organization’s policies and
procedures to deliver accessible products and services. It can be used to measure
current capabilities, identify gaps, and plan improvements. For more details, review
questions and how to comment, see the, link, email announcing the Accessibility Maturity
link, Model update, dot Please comment by 30 September 2025.
